_note: Primary output: 1920×1080 landscape (16:9). All three p-longform formats (vsl/demo/tutorial) produce this canvas; qa_gate uses 'vsl' (the c-shorts-qa-gate landscape check set). Duration band 300–1200 s covers all formats (vsl: 10–20 min; demo: 5–15 min; tutorial: 5–20 min) — the VSL primary is 600–1200 s; a brand may narrow via brand-overrides/<slug>/acceptance.json. Strict, recipe-owned spec read by c-eval-runner. A brand may layer brand-overrides/<slug>/acceptance.json to TIGHTEN thresholds (same id wins; new ids appended). See cfw-skills-pack/docs/skills-audit.md §4.

checks.desc: (a) Background is never fully black at any sampled frame (15/30/45/60/75/90% of runtime) — b-roll, graphics, or avatar fills the frame on every beat; no bare black void mid-video.
(b) On full-avatar beats, the talking head fills the 1920×1080 frame and the complete face is visible — forehead to chin, not cropped, not stretched; no green-screen ghost fringe around the edges.

(c) On pip beats, the framed-inset PIP card (portrait ~304×380, in a bottom corner — alternating left or right across beats, ~72px margin) is fully on-screen with clear margin — not clipped by the frame edge; the speaker is fully contained inside the card. The card is a ROUNDED RECTANGLE with a thin gold (#D4A84C) border and a soft drop shadow — NOT a circle/ellipse and NOT a bare borderless rectangle.

(d) Graphics cards and captions never overlap whichever bottom-corner PIP zone the framed-inset card occupies on that beat (left or right) — the speaker and the gold rim are never obscured by text or card overlays on pip beats.
(e) Background fills the full 1920×1080 frame on every beat — no pillarbox bars, no letterbox bands, no distortion; blurred-fill or FIT compositing covers any aspect-ratio gaps.

(f) When captions are enabled, text is legible with a brand-accent active-word highlight, positioned in the lower band (CAP_TOP=820) and clear of BOTH bottom corners (the framed-inset PIP alternates left↔right) — never entering the PIP card zone and never cut off by the frame edge.

(g) Frame 0 (cover) is the money-shot — a bright, on-brand illustrative beat; not black, not a blank hook frame, not a mid-motion blur. Corroborated by the frame0_not_black luma_floor check (YAVG > 30).
(h) B-roll or GFX cards cover at least the declared broll_coverage target (default 80%) of runtime — no long bare-avatar stretches except the hook (0–30 s) and the final CTA beat (last 60 s).

(i) A branded CTA card is visible within the final 60 seconds of the video — on-screen text with offer line or call-to-action; the video never closes on black or a bare talking-head with no CTA (vsl mandatory, recommended for demo/tutorial).
(j) On avatar-visible beats, lip movement is plausibly in sync with the audio — no obvious multi-second drift or frozen-mouth artifact at any sampled frame.
(k) No green-screen residual fringe visible around the avatar on any beat where colorkey was applied — edges are clean with no green haloing or semi-transparent spill.
